mappings.rationaleAI: The Iron Age covers iron smelting technology, its advantages over bronze, and the spread of ironworking, directly relevant to mining/quarrying and the production of iron from metal ores.
The Industrial Revolution covers coal mining, iron and steel production, directly relevant to mining products and the processing of iron and steel.
Carbon's industrial applications in steel manufacturing directly relate to the production of iron and steel alloys.
Glass science covers the history of glassmaking from ancient Mesopotamia through Venetian Murano to modern applications, directly relevant to the manufacture of glass and glass products.
The geological formation of Britain covers Carboniferous coal measures and mineral resources, directly relevant to the identification and distribution of natural resources and mining products.
Oxygen's discovery and isolation relates to the processing of air to obtain oxygen and other gases.
